name: Steamed Pungent Cod with Cabbage
recipeIngredient: 2 Green onions, sliced into thin strips
recipeInstructions:
Sauce: In a saucepan, heat water and crushed rock sugar until the sugar melts. Add pickle juice, soy sauce, vinegar, and diced pickles. Bring to boil, stirring constantly. Stir in cornstarch paste gradually, continuing to stir until the sauce has the consistency of thin pancake batter. Remove from heat and set aside.
Preparation: Slice the cod filet against the grain about 1/2 inch thick, keeping the slices together. Trim any leafy fringes from the cabbage (save for stock). Cut the cabbage stalks lengthwise into pieces to match the fish slices. Place the cabbage between each slice of fish and press together. Using a broad side of a cleaver, lift the fish and cabbage onto an 10-inch oval serving dish. Sprinkle ginger and sesame oil over the fish.
Steaming: About 15 minutes before serving, bring water in the steamer to boiling. Steam the fish platter for 7-10 minutes, until the fish turns milky white. Meanwhile, reheat the sauce by setting it aside. Remove the fish from the steamer and discard any excess water. Pour the warmed sauce over the fish so it is only partly covered. Garnish with green onions. Serve.
